# Craftsmanship Scales: BRAVE and Critical Thinking

This reference highlights where BRAVE fits in RoleModel's Craftsmanship Radar, focusing on the Critical Thinking dimension and the progression it unlocks.

## BRAVE in the Craftsmanship Progression

| Level | Phase | Critical Thinking |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Novice | Apprentice | Can follow recipes and do basic troubleshooting |
| Apprentice | Apprentice | (developing foundational skills) |
| Resident | Apprentice | **Uses BRAVE framework to deliver value** |
| Sr. Resident | Apprentice | Effectively works through debugging/troubleshooting tasks |
| Developer I | Journeyman | Can figure out straightforward tasks that require some research |
| Developer II | Journeyman | Makes the right tradeoffs to balance personal productivity within project constraints |
| Developer III | Journeyman | Can troubleshoot problems in systems they haven't worked in |
| Sr. Developer | Journeyman | Thinks about issues at a higher level and uses small experiments to iteratively arrive at the best solutions |
| Craftsman | Craftsman | Can lead an Explore to build the right walking skeleton and produce a roadmap |

**BRAVE is the critical thinking milestone for the Resident level** — the point where a developer moves from following instructions to actively validating the value and approach of their work before starting it.

---

## Key Definitions

**Simple task** — Anyone who knows the related technology can potentially diagnose and/or do it: e.g. changing calculations, text, manner of display, adding new properties to an object and supporting methods around it.

**Straightforward task** — Solution requires new classes and non-trivial methods spanning multiple domain objects (and corresponding controllers/views), consistency and responsiveness of user interface, tests are written well and handling edge cases, but clear steps to take for the implementation.

**Significant contributor** — Key team member completing a lot of work on the project over a significant amount of time.

---

## Collaboration Expectations by Level

Understanding who you're working with shapes how the BRAVE conversation should go:

- **Resident**: They can take on straightforward tasks, but will need help getting unblocked. You need a short feedback loop to ensure they're going in the right direction.
- **Sr. Resident**: You point them in the right direction. They can follow patterns in the system.
- **Developer I**: You set guardrails for the task and help them break it down.
- **Developer II**: You can give them ill-defined tasks and they pull the information needed from the customer or another source to deliver well and not get blocked.
- **Developer III**: They own a piece of the system and collaborate with Craftsman on functionality and technical implementation.
